lecture_title: Lecture 7 : New Revolutions in Particle Physics： Basic Concepts

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: None of the candidates show blackboard mathematics, diagrams, or lecture-room layout. They are all exterior campus shots or the Stanford title card, so they are not useful as documentary visual evidence for note writing.

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This is the only candidate in the window that shows the board after the discussion has advanced from coordinate notation to the actual angular-momentum construction. Although Susskind partly occludes the left side, the frame preserves both a vector sketch and the angular-momentum formula layout, making it more useful for note writing than the earlier frames, which show only the definition of the position vector \(\vec R\).
assets.subtitle_excerpt: the angular momentum is a product of the two vectors.
assets.caption_hint: angular momentum from position and momentum vectors

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest combined view of the two useful board elements at this moment: the statement that the raising operator annihilates the top state, and the vertical ladder of magnetic quantum numbers on the right. The writing is stable and legible, and Susskind does not significantly block the relevant mathematics.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Linear length of momentum equals zero, and nothing else.

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_04.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The angular momentum vector is the polar and azimuthal angle
assets.caption_hint: The angular momentum vector is the polar and azimuthal angle
